stocks.name: Enbridge Inc.
stocks.thesis: Record 2025: GAAP earnings $7.1B ($3.23/share, +38% YoY), adjusted EBITDA $20B (+7%), DCF $12.5B (+4%). 31st consecutive dividend increase to $3.88/share (+3%). $39B secured backlog (+35% since Enbridge Day), $14B new projects sanctioned in 2025 including Mainline Optimization, Eiger Express expansion to 3.7 Bcf/d, and renewable power deals with Meta. 20th consecutive year of achieving/exceeding guidance. RBC target raised to $76. Near ATH but valuation justified — add on any pullback to $65-67.
stocks.risks: D/EBITDA at 4.8x — manageable but limited buffer. Ongoing equity dilution from $39B growth capex. Trading near ATH ($74.39) with limited margin of safety at current levels. Best re-entry $65-67 CAD for 5.8-6.0% yield.

stocks.name: Bank of Nova Scotia
stocks.thesis: Q1 2026 earnings released Feb 24: $2.3B net income vs $993M year ago (+132% YoY) — massive beat. Pacific Alliance diversification (LatAm) is contrarian value play. At ~$104 CAD, trading above 200-day MA with fresh earnings catalyst. Add on pullback to $98–101 CAD for yield ~4.4–4.5%.
stocks.risks: Higher international credit exposure (LatAm) vs RY/TD creates more earnings volatility across credit cycles. Not a core sleep-at-night holding at full weight.

stocks.name: TELUS Corporation
stocks.thesis: 5G capex tapering driving FCF surge — record $2.2B FCF in 2025, up 11% YoY with 10%+ CAGR guided through 2028. Committed to 3-8% annual dividend increases from 2026 through 2028. A 9% yield with dividend growth in a telecom with hard infrastructure moat.
stocks.risks: High debt load; TELUS International drag and CRTC regulatory risk remain. Dividend growth lower end of 3-8% range if macro deteriorates.

stocks.name: Magna International Inc.
stocks.thesis: North America's largest auto parts supplier trading at a 20% discount to its 52-week high and a cheap 9.8x normalized P/E. Potential Trump-era North American manufacturing policies are a structural tailwind for this irreplaceable industrial backbone.
stocks.risks: Auto production is cyclical and vulnerable to recession; cross-border tariff complexity could create headwinds despite a large NA footprint.
